title: image is rebuilt when it could be potentially avoided
body:
podman python sdk version: 5.4.0.1
podman cli version: 5.4.2
OS platform: fedora 42 wsl
description: 

I used very straightforward code below
```
    with PodmanClient(base_url=base_url) as client:
        image, _stream = client.images.build(
            path=".", 
            dockerfile="Containerfile",
            tag=TAG,
        )
```
to build this Containerfile:
```
FROM fedora:42
RUN echo 123
```
each time it seems to rebuild the image instead of using the cache(each time I got a new image id).
in comparison, the command podman build works fine with the above containerfile and it reuses cache and yields the same image id.
I also noticed the code didn't rebuild when the Containerfile is simply
```
FROM fedora:42
```

Let me know if I missed something. Thanks.

comments.body: It's been a while, can someone help me with this🙏
Hi @inknos  do you mind taking a look at this, thank you!
I'm not sure if this ancient issue is relevant https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12378?utm_source=chatgpt.com
It's a duplicate of [this one](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/issues/528) I believe. what do you think @nezen ?

> It's a duplicate of [this one](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/issues/528) I believe. what do you think [@nezen](https://github.com/nezen) ?

Yes, you are right. Thanks for letting me know. And thanks to @fulminemizzega for the solution.
